title: [epic] Border Router Implementation
description:
6LBR (6LoWPAN Border Router) connecting LICHEN mesh to IPv6 internet.

Architecture options:

**Option A: Puck + PC (primary, start here)**
- LoRa puck (T-Beam, RAK4631) runs link layer + SCHC
- Linux PC (Pi, NUC, server) runs IPv6 routing + RPL root
- SLIP over USB serial between them
- PC has internet uplink (Ethernet, WiFi, LTE)

**Option B: Standalone (future)**
- Single device with LoRa + Ethernet/WiFi
- RAK WisGate, custom hardware, or ESP32+W5500
- Runs full stack on embedded Linux (OpenWRT/Buildroot)
- PoE for remote deployment

Core functions:
- RPL DODAG root (advertises routes into mesh)
- IPv6 ND proxy (mesh nodes get addresses)
- Prefix delegation from upstream
- NAT64/DNS64 if upstream is IPv4-only (optional)
- Resource Directory (RFC 9176) for CoAP discovery
- NTP/NTS time source for mesh

Components to build:
1. SLIP interface driver (Zephyr side)
2. SLIP interface driver (Linux side, use existing)
3. Border router daemon (RPL root, ND proxy)
4. Integration with Linux networking (ip6tables, routing)
5. systemd service files
6. Configuration management

Dependencies:
- Full LICHEN stack working on puck
- RPL implementation complete
- Local client interface defined (for SLIP framing)

Testing:
- Internet host pings mesh node
- Mesh node pings internet host
- CoAP request from internet to mesh resource
- Mesh node registers with Resource Directory
close_reason: TUN+SLIP↔TUN forwarding loop, gateway SCHC compress/decompress, --no-tun CI flag, systemd unit and example TOML
